Cell Cell Communication.description: CCCdb is a manually curated database of experimentally validated cell–cell communications (CCC) in human and mouse across tissues and phenotypes.
CITEdb is a manually curated database of human cell–cell interactions with structured annotations including context, cell types, and interaction details.

Ligand Receptor Pair.description: CellChatDB is a curated database of ligand–receptor interactions (including cofactors) used by CellChat to infer cell–cell communication networks.
CellPhoneDB is a curated database of human ligand-receptor interaction definitions for cell-cell communication analysis.

connectomeDB2025 is a rigorously curated, open access database of peptide based ligand–receptor interactions with primary experimental evidence, intended for accurate inference of cell–cell communication networks across multiple vertebrate species.

General Database.description: CellMarker 2.0 is an updated, manually curated database of cell markers in human and mouse.
PanglaoDB is a web server for exploring human and mouse scRNA‑seq data and provides a community‑curated cell‑type marker compendium.
Azimuth provides reference atlases and mapping tools for single‑cell datasets.
Query CELLxGENE Census expression and metadata across curated tissues, diseases, organisms, cell types, studies, and population-scale single-cell atlases.
singleCellBase is a manually curated database of cell type–marker gene associations across multiple species.

Cancer Stem Cell.description: BCSCdb is a curated database of cancer stem cell (CSC) biomarkers, therapeutic targets, and biomarker interactions.

T Cell Subtype.description: TCellAtlas provides a large scale human T cell reference and a tool (STCAT) for automatic annotation of T cell subtypes and states at single cell resolution.

Cancer Biomarkers.description: Detect microsatellite instability from sequencing assays, select compatible callers and thresholds, and report MSI-H evidence for oncology and Lynch syndrome.
Calculate and interpret tumor mutational burden with assay-aware denominators, germline and artifact filtering, calibration, and oncology reporting criteria.

Clinical Evidence.description: Verify bounded biomedical facts and multiple-choice claims against authoritative public databases rather than answering database-dependent questions from memory.

Genetic Risk.description: Query the NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog for variant-trait associations, studies, mapped genes, p-values, ancestry, and available summary statistics.
Construct, calibrate, validate, and report ancestry-aware polygenic risk scores using public score catalogs and established PRS methods.

Immunogenetics.description: Type and reconcile HLA alleles from sequencing or imputation workflows and interpret transplant, pharmacogenomic, neoantigen, and disease-association use cases.

Pharmacogenomics.description: Query ClinPGx, CPIC, DPWG, and related pharmacogenomic evidence and translate star alleles into phenotypes and genotype-guided prescribing guidance.

Phenotypes.description: Human Phenotype Ontology (HPO) organizes and defines human disease phenotypes and supports phenotype-driven genomic analysis.

Therapeutic Targets.description: ChEMBL is a manually curated bioactivity database of small molecules, assays, targets, and approved or clinical drugs.
DrugBank is a comprehensive drug database combining chemical, pharmacological, target, and clinical information for approved and investigational drugs.
openFDA is the FDA open-data platform providing API access to public drug, device, food, tobacco, and regulatory datasets.
OpenTargets Platform integrates human genetics, omics, literature, and chemistry data to prioritize target-disease associations.

Variants.description: Interpret germline and somatic variants using ACMG/AMP, ClinGen SVI, Bayesian evidence, PVS1, computational, functional, and tumor-tier frameworks.
Query ClinVar clinical significance, review status, submitter evidence, ClinGen expert-panel curation, and germline or somatic interpretations.
Resolve dbSNP rsIDs and merge histories and convert among rsID, SPDI, HGVS, and VCF representations using current NCBI records.
Query gnomAD population frequencies, filtering allele frequencies, constraint metrics, structural variants, mitochondrial variants, and release-specific evidence.
Query myvariant.info for versioned, batched aggregation of ClinVar, gnomAD, dbSNP, dbNSFP, COSMIC, CADD, CIViC, and related variant annotations.
Prioritize rare-disease variants using inheritance, phasing, phenotype similarity, gene-disease validity, mosaic evidence, and secondary-findings guidance.

Comparative Genomics.description: Retrieve and compare precomputed ortholog calls from Ensembl Compara, OrthoDB, OMA, eggNOG, PANTHER, KEGG Orthology, and related resources.

Ncbi Entrez.description: Search, link, fetch, and batch-download records across NCBI Entrez databases with history-server pagination, rate limiting, retries, and integrity checks.

Ncbi Datasets.description: Retrieve NCBI genomes, genes, orthologs, and related metadata with the Datasets v2 CLI using accession-driven and checksum-validated workflows.

Sequence Retrieval.description: Retrieve DNA, RNA, transcript, and protein sequences across NCBI and ENA with accession disambiguation and curated-record preference.

Gene Annotation.description: Bulk-query Ensembl and compatible BioMart services for identifier mapping, genomic coordinates, transcript structure, and cross-species ortholog tables.
Ensembl is a genome annotation and browser resource providing gene, transcript, exon, comparative genomics, and cross-reference data.

Query current NCBI Gene records by symbol, Gene ID, organism, or search expression. Use for gene summaries, aliases, nomenclature, chromosome and map location, genomic coordinates, and identifier verification through NCBI E-utilities.

Human Chromosome Cytogenetic Bands.description: MSigDB C1 positional gene sets organize genes by chromosomal cytogenetic bands and genomic regions.

Circrna.description: circRNADb is a comprehensive database of human circular RNAs with genomic annotation, exon structure, sequence, IRES, and ORF/protein-coding evidence.

Snorna.description: snoDB 2.0 is an interactive database of human small nucleolar RNAs with features, genomic locations, host genes, targets, and abundance links.

Blast.description: Run remote NCBI BLAST or local BLAST+ searches, select programs and databases, build custom databases, and retrieve reproducible sequence-similarity results.

Gene Ontology.description: Gene Ontology (GO) provides structured knowledge of gene functions.

Kegg.description: KEGG provides pathway maps representing molecular interaction and reaction networks.

Progeny.description: PROGENy is a pathway activity inference method based on perturbation‑response gene signatures.

Reactome.description: Reactome is a free, expert-curated biological pathway database covering signaling, metabolism, reactions, and molecular events.

Wikipathways.description: WikiPathways is an open, community curated pathway database.

Cell Senescence.description: CellAge is a manually curated database of human genes associated with cellular senescence.

Epithelial Mesenchymal Transition.description: dbEMT 2.0 is a curated database of epithelial–mesenchymal transition (EMT) related genes with experimental evidence.
EMTome is a pan‑cancer resource of epithelial–mesenchymal transition (EMT) gene signatures and related multi‑omics annotations.

Regulated Cell Death.description: FerrDb V3 is a manually curated, open access database of regulated cell death (RCD) regulators and RCD–disease associations.

Cancer Cell Program.description: This resource summarizes meta programs (co regulated gene sets) identified from the Curated Cancer Cell Atlas (3CA) study of transcriptional intratumour heterogeneity.
CancerSEA is a cancer single‑cell functional state atlas that provides gene signatures for 14 functional states (e.g., EMT, proliferation, stemness).

Signature.description: ImmuneSigDB is a curated collection of immunologic gene signatures derived from human and mouse immunology studies.
MSigDB Hallmark is a curated collection of 50 gene sets representing well-defined biological processes.
Extract, assign, and interpret COSMIC somatic mutational signatures across SBS, DBS, indel, copy-number, and structural-variant profiles.
VAX is a curated collection of vaccine response gene signatures from the Human Immunology Project Consortium (HIPC).

Chemical Compounds.description: Resolve chemical identities and retrieve structures, identifiers, properties, and stereochemistry across PubChem, ChEMBL, OPSIN, and related compound sources.
Search ZINC purchasable compounds by identifier or structure and retrieve docking-ready structures and analogs for virtual screening.

Metabolomics.description: HMDB is a comprehensive human metabolome database covering metabolite structures, pathways, concentrations, diseases, spectra, and related proteins.
MACdb is a curated knowledgebase of cancer–metabolite associations derived from manual curation of published studies.
PubChem is a public chemistry database maintained by NCBI with large-scale compound, substance, and bioassay information.

Protein Annotation.description: UniProtKB is a comprehensive protein sequence and functional annotation knowledgebase.

Protein Homology.description: Detect distant protein homologs using iterative profiles, HMMs, fast sequence search, and structure-aware Foldseek workflows when standard BLAST is insufficient.

Protein Protein Interaction.description: Select and query complementary interaction resources including STRING, BioGRID, IntAct, SIGNOR, Reactome, HuRI, HuMAP, OmniPath, ConsensusPathDB, and DIP.
STRING is a database of known and predicted protein-protein associations integrating experiments, curated databases, co-expression, and text mining.

Protein Structure.description: AlphaFold Protein Structure Database provides predicted protein structures and confidence metrics for proteins with UniProt identifiers.

Circrna Regulation.description: RegNetwork is an open source gene regulatory network (GRN) database that curates regulatory relationships among transcription factors (TFs), microRNAs (miRNAs), and genes in human and mouse, and provides a confidence scoring system in the 2025 update.

Lncrna Regulation.description: RegNetwork is an open source gene regulatory network (GRN) database that curates regulatory relationships among transcription factors (TFs), microRNAs (miRNAs), and genes in human and mouse, and provides a confidence scoring system in the 2025 update.

Mirna Regulation.description: miRDB provides predicted functional microRNA (miRNA) targets.
miRTarBase is a curated database of experimentally validated microRNA–target interactions (MTIs).

RegNetwork is an open source gene regulatory network (GRN) database that curates regulatory relationships among transcription factors (TFs), microRNAs (miRNAs), and genes in human and mouse, and provides a confidence scoring system in the 2025 update.

Rbp Regulation.description: KnockRBP is a multi omics database that catalogs regulatory changes caused by genetic perturbation of RNA binding proteins (RBPs).

Rna Modification Protein Wer.description: RM2Target v2.0 is a curated database of regulatory associations between RNA modification proteins (writers, erasers, readers; WERs) and their target genes.

Snorna Guided Rrna Modification.description: snoDB 2.0 provides curated information on human snoRNAs and their guided rRNA chemical modifications.

Transcription Factor.description: AnimalTFDB 4.0 is a comprehensive animal transcription factor database with TF families, cofactors, and regulatory annotations.
CollecTRI is a meta resource of transcription factor (TF)–target gene interactions used to build signed TF regulons for estimating TF activity.
GTRD (Gene Transcription Regulation Database) provides uniformly processed transcription regulation datasets and TF binding site annotations.

RegNetwork is an open source gene regulatory network (GRN) database that curates regulatory relationships among transcription factors (TFs), microRNAs (miRNAs), and genes in human and mouse, and provides a confidence scoring system in the 2025 update.
TF-Marker is a manually curated database of transcription factors and related markers for specific human cell and tissue types.

TRRUST (Transcriptional Regulatory Relationships Unraveled by Sentence based Text mining) is a reference database of transcription factor (TF)–target interactions constructed through sentence based text mining of PubMed literature, followed by manual curation.

Cross Domain.description: Map scientific questions to suitable public cohorts, studies, repositories, and dataset designs and evaluate candidate datasets before retrieval.

Metabolomics.description: Metabolomics Workbench is a public repository for metabolomics studies, experimental metadata, standards, protocols, and analysis resources.

Proteomics.description: Find and evaluate public proteomics datasets in ProteomeXchange, MassIVE, and related repositories by species, instrument, protocol, accession, and modification.

Sequencing.description: Search and retrieve European Nucleotide Archive sequences, reads, assemblies, analyses, and metadata through Portal, Browser, and file endpoints.
Locate and download NCBI SRA raw sequencing runs through SRA Toolkit, ENA, or cloud mirrors with accession, layout, checksum, and scratch-space safeguards.

Structural Biology.description: Protein Data Bank (PDB) is the primary archive of experimentally determined 3D structures of proteins, nucleic acids, and macromolecular complexes.
Retrieve and compare experimental and predicted protein structures across RCSB PDB, PDBe, and AlphaFold with quality and identity checks.

Transcriptomics.description: Find and assess expression and omics datasets across ArrayExpress, BioStudies, GEO, and related repositories by organism, tissue, condition, and study design.
Search and retrieve NCBI GEO expression studies, samples, platforms, processed matrices, supplementary files, and linked raw sequencing accessions.

Clinical Trials.description: Search ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 by condition, intervention, location, phase, status, sponsor, or identifier and retrieve structured trial records.

Patents.description: Search USPTO patent, trademark, examination-history, assignment, citation, and office-action APIs for biomedical intellectual-property evidence.

Publications.description: Search and retrieve PubMed records with MeSH-aware Boolean queries, NCBI E-utilities, pagination, batching, and reproducible citation metadata.
